news.title: Chip Rebound Fizzles, Hammers Nasdaq
news.summary: The Nasdaq tumbled on Tuesday after a rebound in semiconductor stocks fizzled out overnight.  The Dow dropped 131 points, or 0.3%.  After chips rebounded on Monday, selling in memory stocks in South Korea bled into U.S. trading.

news.title: Oil Jumps After U.S. Bars Iranian Oil Sales
news.summary: Oil prices jumped in late trading Tuesday after the Treasury Department revoked a waiver that allowed Iran to sell oil, signaling a new test for stop-and-start peace talks between the countries.  The U.S. in June waived restrictions on Iranian oil sales as part of a memorandum of understanding that halted fighting between the countries for 60 days.

news.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Losses Mount; Pharma Names See Strength (Live Coverage)
news.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes weakened in the final hour of trading Tuesday, as recent tech winners Micron Technology and Sandisk plunged.  Meanwhile, IBM was a Dow leader and pharmaceutical names topped the IBD 50 on the stock market today.  The Dow declined 0.3%, falling below the 53,000 level after closing above it for the first time on Monday.
